glitches.title: Hyper Dash (HD)
glitches.alt_names: Wave Dash (like in Celeste), Long Jump, Collision Jump, Ricochet
glitches.how_to: From the air (usually after a jump), dash into a walkable surface and quickly press jump. You'll be launched with the speed of the dash on a long distance, like a boosted bunny hop. It is possible on flat surfaces: the flatter it is, the more delay it needs between dash and jump.

variations.text: Element Hyper Dash (Poison/Steam/Nitro...) - As water form, do an Hyper Dash into a liquid to get the corresponding boost. The Poison version seems to be the most useful for now.

glitches.note: Often used in the run, especially in the intro before getting the Form Dash. Seems conditioned to a higher framerate (ideally, 120fps no vsync).

glitches.title: Jump Switch (JS)
glitches.how_to: As water form, jump and switch at the same time, or at least in that order, to get a small boost up.
glitches.note: Mostly used in speedrun as the poison variation, but the basic water Jump Switch still gains a small boost and allows to chain very fast jumps (See Form Dash notes).

variations.text: Poison Jump Switch - When releasing the poison by jumping, delay your transformation to receive a big momentum up as One.

glitches.title: Hyper Switch (HS)
glitches.how_to: Combination of Hyper Dash and Jump Switch - Starting as water form in the air, dash into a surface and immediately jump > switch into One.
glitches.note: Allows to get a bit more height and distance with the Hyper Dash. Can help with jumps like this one.

variations.text: Poison Hyper Switch - Combination of Poison Hyper Dash and Poison Jump Switch - Water form > Hyper Dash into Poison > after jumping, delay the transfo like for Poison Jump Switch > get a big boost up and forward.

glitches.title: Form Dash (FD)
glitches.how_to: Dash just before a transformation to reach a very high speed. Usually stands for the aerial version, but works on the ground (quickly alternating each input).

variations.text: Form Dash Poison (FDP) - Press jump > switch > dash (with a precise timing). This will perform a Form Dash without switching to One, but staying as water form. Can be done to keep an item stored while performing a Form Dash.

glitches.note: Very often used in the run after the intro. To chain them properly, start as water form > Jump Switch (jump>switch) > Form Dash (dash>switch) > as soon as you land as water form, repeat this cycle (it can get very fast).

glitches.title: Jump Kick (JK)
glitches.how_to: From the air (usually quickly after a jump), do the kick move into a geometry corner to gain momentum up.
glitches.note: This specific clip is from the older demo. It still remains possible but has been nerfed since then. Conditioned to a higher framerate (ideally, 120fps no vsync).

glitches.title: Cutscene Skip (CS)
glitches.how_to: From the main menu, choose new game > difficulty and when you validate, quickly press down and enter. What it does is that for a brief moment after starting, you're still able to navigate through the menu: going down and validating makes you choose CONTINUE. Right after that, hold your direction forward, dash, jump and attack. You should be able to hear these actions during the black-screen (and feel a vibration after the attack).
glitches.extra_content:
To summarize:

Enter > Down > Enter > Hold stick forward > Dash > Jump > Attack
glitches.note: It's at the base of many important glitches like the next ones (Void Cancel, Infinite Dash, etc.). It is a key glitch that allowed us along with an incredible teamwork to get a WR (3:57.04) runned by @Hulien. The timings are not so quick and very precise, it is recommended trying different timings and picking the one that suits your playstyle or watch (and listen to) this. You know you did it right if One doesn't talk right after the cutscene and there is no camera movement. It is highly recommended to have the sound of the game turned on, so you can hear your inputs.

glitches.title: Void Cancel (VC)
glitches.how_to: Through the Cutscene Skip method, fall until the game tries to respawn you - it will fail and you'll instead continuously receive fall damage. Go back to the menu and continue. You will now be able to fall forever, no void, no respawn.
glitches.note: This glitch allows you to bypass the fall limit that respawns you on the ground. You will be able to cover great vertical and horizontal distances in the air. This state resists everything, even rebooting the game. Just be careful not to fall for too long or you'll get a "fatal error" crash.
glitches.button_text: Read the Complete Void Cancel Guide

glitches.title: Infinite Dash (ID)
glitches.how_to: Through the Cutscene Skip method, restart from checkpoint (it should not replace you anywhere else, the CS doesn't register the first hard checkpoint) and start mashing dashes.

variations.text: Void Infinite Dash (VC not active) - You will get stuck in the air continuously losing HP but able to "fly" towards your destination (restarting checkpoint allows you to refill your HP and potion).
Voidless Infinite Dash (VC active) - You will be able to naturally infinite dash without losing HP.

Respawn Infinite Dash (No CS needed) - This alternate version can work without specific setup. Just mash the dash after respawning from a fall/void. Ideally, do it from a respawn that gets you in the air (like shown in the clip). This can't last very long and the game will make you void pretty fast.

glitches.title: Jojo Glitch
glitches.alt_names: Tube Glitch, Flying Glitch, Air-Lock Glitch
glitches.how_to: Go into a water tube and simply restart from checkpoint (it needs to be in the same level). This seems to disturb the transition from ground to air and locks you into a walking state, unable to jump. You'll be standing onto an invisible floor with the possibility to work your hitbox up or down by entering in contact with the environment. Find the ideal collisions in the level design to navigate in the available space. It allows the Volcano Skip (follow this routing).
glitches.note: The best spot to perform this is the portal going from Bastion to Volcano, in the underwater biome (there is a checkpoint just next to the first tubes). When active, the fastest way to move horizontally is with the ground version of the Form Dash (quickly alternating dash and switch). The best way to go up seems to be by mashing the switch (no dash) while facing an inclined wall.

glitches.title: Portal Glitch
glitches.alt_names: Portal Wrong Load, Portal Escape Glitch
glitches.how_to: Previously done thanks to the "restart from checkpoint" - patched. Still possible thanks to the glitch form (purple). Place a teleportation mark out of the portal, turn the camera away from this point, stand on the central button to activate the portal but quickly teleport back to the mark. The camera will turn to aim the mark you left, leaving enough time for the portal to activate just before you get teleported. Once escaped, the level will still unload and switch to the targeted one, leaving you unprotected and free for a few seconds...
glitches.note: Can be used to unload walls and go OOB. Opening the start menu at a specific moment can allow the new level to load while suspending your position.

glitches.title: Glitch²
glitches.how_to: As One, get affected by the glitch liquid but dash away from the edge of the pool to fall/void during the animation. After respawning, you'll be insensible to other liquids and to enemy attacks.
glitches.note: This is a hidden liquid/color still accessible in the demo, giving us a glimpse on the future puzzles and exploits we might be able to do in the full game... No real use found yet but great potential for the full game and its most intense combat phases.

glitches.title: Infinite Core Glitch
glitches.card_style: border: 2px solid #ff6b9d; background: linear-gradient(135deg, rgba(255, 107, 157, 0.05), rgba(0, 245, 255, 0.03));
glitches.note: ⚠️ WORK IN PROGRESS: We are currently working on the return of the Infinite Core Glitch!

glitches.title: Point Zero
glitches.card_style: border: 2px solid #ff6b9d; background: linear-gradient(135deg, rgba(255, 107, 157, 0.05), rgba(0, 245, 255, 0.03));
glitches.how_to: What we call the "Point Zero" is located at certain coordinates far from the levels, and can be reached by quickly coming back to the menu (keep mashing to continue quickly). The first frame when One spawns in the game, she will be standing there onto a small platform, before being replaced at the last soft checkpoint.
glitches.extra_content: If you quickly go back to the menu twice in a row, you can actually place your soft checkpoint on the Point Zero and stay there. From that, many things might be possible!
